assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Output line 7: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' — identical to reference line 16.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: Output line 8: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new row with serena_ui as Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: Output lines 12-16 match reference lines 20-25 exactly: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has '## Preserved (unchanged from existing configuration)' listing trustify-backend, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, Bug Configuration, and '## Added (new entries)' listing trustify-ui and serena_ui limitations.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 11: '| serena_backend | Already configured | Found in existing Repository Registry with repository trustify-backend |' and line 12: '| serena_ui | Newly discovered | Found in MCP tools but not in Repository Registry; added as trustify-ui |'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: Output lines 19-33 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), the mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol example, and the serena_backend limitation verbatim from the reference. Only addition is line 34: '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: Output lines 37-40: '- Bug issue type ID: 10001', '- Bug template: docs/bug-template.md', '- Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks' — identical to reference lines 44-47. changes-log.md lists Bug Configuration under 'Preserved (unchanged from existing configuration)' indicating it is up to date.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 20-21: 'User was asked whether to enable security triage' and 'User declined -- Security Configuration section not added'. changes-log.md line 37: 'User declined opt-in for security triage -- section omitted'. claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section.
